name: Cults
description:
'''Cults''' is an American [[indie pop]] band formed in [[New York City]] in 2010, comprising multi-instrumentalists Madeline Follin (born November 1988) and Brian Oblivion (born 1989). The band first rose to prominence after the release of their debut extended play, ''Cults 7"'' (2010), which was released on their [[Bandcamp]] page. They signed with English singer [[Lily Allen]]'s record label [[In the Name Of (record label)|In the Name Of]], an imprint of [[Sony Music]], to release their [[Cults (album)|eponymous debut studio album]] (2011). Their song "Bad Things" was sampled by American rapper [[J. Cole]] for his 2013 single "[[She Knows (J. Cole song)|She Knows]]", on which they were credited as featured artists.

The band has since released another four albums: [[Static (Cults album)|''Static'']] (2013), ''[[Offering (Cults album)|Offering]]'' (2017), ''[[Host (Cults album)|Host]]'' (2020) and ''[[To the Ghosts]]'' (2024), as well as an EP, ''Motels'' (2018).
